layman_headline: Market fear is high but no clean entry exists — patience is the best trade right now.
layman_explanation: The crypto market is in a fearful state with a Fear & Greed index of 28. Bitcoin is holding steady around $64,600, but most altcoins are in downtrends with bearish chart structures. While Ethereum and a few others show early signs of stabilization after recent drops, none of the candidates present a high-conviction entry with good risk-reward right now. The best approach is to wait for a clearer reversal signal rather than catch a falling knife.
layman_action: Stay on the sidelines for now. Keep cash ready and watch for Ethereum to pull back to the $1,800 support level — if it holds and bounces with volume, that could be a much better entry point.
expert_reasoning: Every swing candidate has a bearish chart bias score (-0.45 to -2.0), with all trading below their major moving averages. BONK's RSI at 31 is the most oversold but its chart is strongly bearish (-2.0) with Three Black Crows — a classic falling knife. ETH shows 5-day momentum recovery (+17.2%) and RSI at 39.4, but it's still below all MAs with a -0.7 chart bias. BTC dominance at 56.5% continues to suppress alt performance. The Fear & Greed index at 28 creates contrarian opportunity eventually, but the flat trend (week avg 25.7) suggests we haven't seen capitulation. I'd rather wait for ETH to retest $1,800 support with a bullish candle confirmation before entering. Risk-reward at current levels (~1.4x to resistance) is inadequate for a swing entry.
risk_factors: BTC weekend risk with geopolitical oil tensions could trigger a broad market selloff dragging all alts lower
BTC dominance at 56.5% and rising means capital continues rotating out of alts into Bitcoin
Fear & Greed flat at 25-28 for a week suggests no capitulation bottom yet — could go lower
watch_list: ETH price action at $1,800 support — a bounce with volume spike would trigger a BUY signal
Fear & Greed index — a drop below 20 or a sharp reversal above 35 would signal sentiment shift
ZEC momentum — up 8.7% on 7d with Zcash scaling news; if it pulls back to support could become a candidate
market_context: The crypto market sits in a fear regime (F&G 28) with BTC relatively stable at $64.6K but altcoins broadly weak. High BTC dominance (56.5%) and bearish chart structures across candidates make this a poor environment for alt swing entries.
context_banner: Fear-driven market with universally bearish alt charts — disciplined patience beats premature entries.

key_signals.description: ETH RSI at 39.4 is approaching oversold territory, our most reliable signal (80% win rate), but hasn't triggered the sub-30 threshold yet — watching closely.
Fear & Greed at 28 with flat weekly trend (25.7 avg) signals persistent fear without capitulation — not yet contrarian enough for high-conviction entry.
All candidates show bearish chart bias (-0.45 to -2.0) with prices below major moving averages — no bullish reversal patterns confirmed on any candidate.
Weekend geopolitical risk with 20% of world's oil supply in balance creates potential for sudden BTC drawdown that would drag alts further.
ETH derivatives reset narrative and DePIN/gaming rebound coverage suggest smart money positioning for next leg, but timing remains uncertain.
